sub: Warm questions — built so someone actually feels heard.

1.name: Open
q: What's a small thing that happened today you haven't told anyone yet?
What's something you're quietly proud of this week that nobody's asked about?
What's a rule you live by that you've never actually said out loud before?
What do people assume about you that's just wrong?
Who's someone who believed in you before you believed in yourself?
What's a smell, sound, or texture that calms you down almost instantly?
What's a small kindness from a stranger that you still think about?
What's one thing about the person across from you that you already like?
What's the most useless skill you're secretly excellent at?
What's an opinion you hold that would surprise the people who think they know you best?
When's the last time you laughed so hard it actually hurt?
What's a small thing you do for other people that you don't think they've ever noticed?
What's the best advice you've ever ignored, on purpose?
Walk into any room — what's the first thing you notice?
What's something you were sure you'd hate and turned out to love?
Who did something for you this week that you still haven't thanked them for?
What's a small thing you've never grown out of, and don't want to?
What's something you're better at than you let on?
What's the boldest thing on your bucket list that you actually intend to do?
What's a small tradition that's just yours — nobody else even knows you keep it?
If tonight had a soundtrack, what's the first song on it?
What's a moment this year that felt like it belonged in a movie, with you in the lead?
What's the last thing you searched that says way too much about your week?
What do you order without even looking at the menu?

2.name: Deeper
q: Who do you wish you told 'thank you' or 'I love you' to more often?
What's a fear you've gone quieter about as you've gotten older?
What's something you needed to hear when you were younger that nobody said?
What do you do, quietly, when you're trying to feel better and no one's watching?
What's a memory that still gets you, every time you think about it?
Is 'home' a place for you right now, or a person?
When do you feel lonely, even around people?
What's the gap between who you are and who people think you are?
What version of yourself are you glad you've outgrown?
What's something you're proud of that would never make it onto a resume?
What's a belief about yourself you're slowly trying to let go of?
What's something you needed more of growing up — attention, space, praise, quiet?
When did you last genuinely surprise yourself?
What's a way you've changed that the people who knew you at eighteen wouldn't recognize?
What's something you do to feel in control when everything else isn't?
What's a family story that explains more about you than you'd like it to?
What's the real difference between the version of you new people meet and the version you actually are?
What's something you've forgiven someone for but never actually told them?
What's a small ache you carry that most people wouldn't guess is there?
What's something you keep almost telling people, and then don't?
Who's someone you've drifted from that you still think about?
What's a way you show love that people might not clock as love?
What's something you privately worry might be true about you?
What do you wish someone would just notice about you, without you having to say it?

3.name: Close
q: Say one true thing about yourself, out loud, that you don't say very often.
Look at them. Don't say anything for a second. Then tell them what you see.
What's something you've never told anyone because you thought it'd change how they saw you?
Finish this out loud, to them — 'Something I appreciate about you that I don't think I've said is…'
What's a moment — tonight, this week, or in your life — you'd call sacred, if you let yourself use that word?
What do you hope they remember about tonight — not what you said, but how you made them feel?
Tell them one thing you've noticed about them tonight that you haven't said out loud yet. Let them just take it in before you say anything else.
When did you last cry in front of someone?
What's something about tonight you already know you don't want to forget?
If tonight was the only night you ever got to know this person, what would you make sure they knew?
What's the truest compliment anyone could give you — the one that would actually land, not just sound nice?
What does it feel like, right now, to be looked at like this?
What's something you're still a little afraid to want?~After they answer, tell them the one part that actually stayed with you.
What's something you still wish someone would just say to you?~Once they've answered, say it to them — for real, like you mean it.
What do you think people get wrong about you?~Once they've answered, tell them what you actually see instead — the true version.
What's a fear about the future you don't usually say out loud?~After they answer, tell them one reason you think they'll be okay anyway.
What's a compliment you've never fully believed, even when someone meant it?~After they answer, tell them exactly why you believe it's true.
Is there something you're proud of that's never really been given the credit it deserves?~After they answer, give them the credit — specifically, out loud.
When have you felt invisible, even around people who were supposed to know you?~After they answer, tell them one specific way you see them right now.
What's something about yourself that's taken you the longest to forgive?~After they answer, tell them: if you'd been in their shoes, you'd forgive them for it in a heartbeat.
What's a version of 'I love you' you find easier to show than to say?~After they answer, tell them you've noticed — and when.
What's something you once needed someone to fight for, and nobody did?~After they answer, tell them: you would have.
What's a question about yourself you're honestly afraid of the answer to?~You don't have to answer it tonight — just tell them what the question is, and let it sit there between you.
When did you last feel proud of who you are, not just what you did?~After they answer, tell them: that's exactly who you've been sitting across from tonight.

wild: Hold eye contact for ten seconds. No talking. Then say the first word that comes to you.
Give them a genuine compliment — the kind you'd usually keep to yourself.
Sit in silence together for fifteen seconds. Actually notice what that feels like.
Point at something they're wearing, carrying, or holding, and ask for the real story behind it.
Trade a small secret. Nothing dramatic — just something true. You go first.
Play the song that matches this exact moment. No explaining it, just play it.
Describe the person across from you to a stranger, in one sentence.
Finish this out loud: 'What I'm not saying is…'
Talk for sixty seconds straight about whatever's actually on your mind. They just listen — no jumping in, no fixing it, not even a question until the minute's up.
Name three actual, specific things you're grateful this person did, said, or is. No deflecting with a joke.
After their next answer, say back what you actually heard before you reply with your own. Not 'same' — the real thing, in your own words.
Raise whatever's in your hand and give them an actual, specific toast. Not 'cheers' — a real one, out loud.
Both say one true thing about yourselves you don't say often — but you have to look at each other the whole time, not the table.
Ask them one question you've been curious about all night but haven't asked yet.
final: Look at each other properly for a second — not mid-sentence, not through a screen. Just look.
Each of you, out loud: one specific thing about tonight — or about them — you don't want to forget.
Say thank you for it, plainly, no joke to hide behind. And mean it.
